content.text: Al-Muwaṭṭaʾ of Imam Malik is one of the earliest and most influential collections of Hadith and Islamic jurisprudence. It was compiled by Imām Mālik ibn Anas (raḥimahullāh) and contains hadith, statements of the Companions, opinions of the Tābiʿīn, and the legal practice of the people of Madinah.

Imām Mālik ibn Anas ibn Mālik al-Aṣbaḥī (93–179 AH / 711–795 CE) was born in Madinah. He became one of the greatest scholars of Islam and the founder of the Mālikī school of jurisprudence. He spent his life teaching Hadith and Fiqh in the Prophet's Mosque and was renowned for his knowledge, piety and adherence to the Sunnah.
Imam Malik studied under many eminent scholars of Madinah, including Nāfiʿ, Ibn Shihāb az-Zuhrī, Yahyā ibn Saʿīd al-Anṣārī, Rabīʿah ar-Ra'y and Jaʿfar aṣ-Ṣādiq.
Among his famous students were Imam ash-Shāfiʿī, Abdullah ibn Wahb, Abdullah ibn al-Qāsim, Yahyā ibn Yahyā al-Laythī and Ashhab ibn Abdul Aziz.

Imam Malik spent around forty years refining Al-Muwaṭṭaʾ. The collection contains approximately 1,700 narrations, depending on the recension, including marfuʿ hadith, statements of the Companions, sayings of the Tābiʿīn and legal opinions. It is arranged according to chapters of Islamic jurisprudence.

items: One of the earliest surviving Hadith collections.
Combines Hadith with Fiqh and the practice of the people of Madinah.
Compiled and refined over several decades.
Highly authentic and respected by scholars.
Organized according to chapters of Islamic jurisprudence.
Includes hadith, Companion narrations and Tābiʿīn opinions.
Served as a foundation for the Mālikī school of law.

content.text: Imām Mālik passed away in Madinah in 179 AH (795 CE) and was buried in Jannat al-Baqi. His scholarship and his Muwaṭṭaʾ continue to be studied throughout the Muslim world.
